mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO level vs 20-day forward return falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5%, it signals tightening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which typically boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in instances with UNRATE < 6.5% are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or hit rate < 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is accommodative (low Fed funds rate) and industrial production is expanding, the combination supports equity valuations through lower discount rates and improved cash flows, leading to positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1% and INDPRO index above 100
failure_condition: In periods where trigger conditions are met, 20-day forward returns are not significantly different from unconditional mean, or interaction term in regression is insignificant at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (Fed funds rate rising for three consecutive months), rising inflation increases uncertainty about future rate hikes, depressing equity valuations and causing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased for three consecutive months
failure_condition: In tightening regimes, the correlation between CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward returns is not negative, or the mean return during tightening with rising CPIAUCSL is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following the release.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS vs forward 5-day returns below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1 million units annualized, it signals robust housing market activity with positive spillovers to consumption and investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months with HOUST > 1M, 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When employment growth and industrial production both expand month-over-month, it confirms a broad-based economic expansion that strongly boosts corporate profits and equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase and INDPRO month-over-month increase
failure_condition: When both signals are positive, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly higher than the unconditional average (t-test p>0.05), or interaction term is not significant at p<0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a rising inflation regime with year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth exceeding 2%, the Federal Reserve is expected to tighten policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 2%, 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative.
